channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas says it will free 3 more hostages as planned, paving the way to resolve ceasefire dispute
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas said Thursday it would release three more Israeli hostages as planned, paving the way toward resolving a major dispute over the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former Hamas hostage's mom says daughter was forced to fake her death for a propaganda video
item.description: Orly Gilboa, mother of former Hamas hostage Daniella Gilboa, said her daughter was forced to fake her own death and was covered in debris for a propaganda video.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said. The blast occurred at the food court on the 12th floor of the Shin Kong Mitsukoshi department store in Taichung city, the Taichung Fire Bureau said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO is in disarray after the US announces that its security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In just one speech by U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth this week, the most powerful member of NATO has thrown the world’s biggest military alliance into disarray, raising troubling questions about America’s commitment to European security.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saudi educator known for charity and prisoner work wins $1 million Global Teacher Prize
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A Saudi educator known for his charity work and instructing prisoners won the $1 million Global Teacher Prize on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and MALAK HARB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How South Korea's Constitutional Court is deciding on the president's future
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol is putting up a desperate fight for his political life at Seoul’s Constitutional Court after being impeached and arrested for his short-lived imposition of martial law last year.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With doors closed to the US, asylum-seekers turn to their Plan B: A new life in Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — When Angelica Delgado took a one-way flight to Mexico as she fled Cuba in December, she was set on seeking asylum in the United States. But after President Donald Trump effectively slammed the door on asylum-seekers crossing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Modi is meeting with Trump in a visit meant to boost the US-India relationship and avoid tariffs
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has met with Trump national security adviser Mike Waltz ahead of his meeting with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Canada’s new fentanyl czar says goal is to totally stop the drug from entering the US
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — The man appointed by the Canadian government to help stop fentanyl from entering the United States said his goal is to totally eliminate the drug crossing the border.
item.dc:creator: JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Seoul says North Korea is destroying facility that hosted reunions of war-separated families
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea is demolishing a South Korea-built property that had been used to host reunions of families separated during the 1950-53 Korean War, the South’s government said Thursday, as it continues to eliminate symbols of engagement between the war-divided rivals.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A joke petition seeks to help Denmark buy California as Trump eyes Greenland
item.description: Is California for sale? A lighthearted petition to buy the state of California for Denmark prompted more than 200,000 signatures by Wednesday in response to President Donald Trump’s talk about taking control of Greenland, a vast and mineral-rich Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steel tariffs could disrupt international bridge expansion
item.description: The steel 2-lane Starr-Camargo International Bridge in Rio Grande City, Texas, is gearing up for an expansion of lanes, however they might not be steel after 25% tariffs were imposed by the Trump administration, the bridge's owner Sam Vale told Border Report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: At least 10 hospitalized in fire at Rio de Janeiro's Carnival costume factory
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — A fire Wednesday at a Rio de Janeiro factory that produces costumes for the city's iconic Carnival left 10 people hospitalized, Rio’s state health department said, casting a shadow over preparations for one of the world’s biggest shows.
item.dc:creator: DIARLEI RODRIGUES and ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he expects to meet with Putin in Saudi Arabia
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday he expects to meet in person with Russian President Vladimir Putin multiple times, suggesting they are likely to meet first in Saudi Arabia. “We ultimately expect to meet,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: In Ecuador's highlands, an Indigenous community faces choice between 2 candidates they don't trust
item.description: COCHAPAMBA, Ecuador (AP) — The two candidates vying for Ecuador’s presidency in a second-round run-off never campaigned in Cochapamba. Their faces are nowhere to be seen in this remote Indigenous community in the Ecuadorian highlands.
item.dc:creator: SARA ESPAÑA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austrian far-right leader's efforts to form a new government collapse
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian far-right leader Herbert Kickl's efforts to form a coalition government with a conservative party collapsed in mutual recriminations on Wednesday, more than four months after his party won a national election.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protests after 2 sudden deaths force closure of hospital trauma unit in Central African Republic
item.description: BANGUI, Central African Republic (AP) — The sudden closure in the Central African Republic of the orthopedic trauma surgery unit at the capital 's main hospital following the deaths of two people, including a government minister's niece, has sparked concern among patients and relatives.
item.dc:creator: JEAN-FERNAND KOENA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Egypt and Qatar are working to salvage the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: Egyptian and Qatari mediators were working to salvage the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas on Wednesday, which risks being derailed because of a dispute between the two sides, according to Egypt’s state-run Al-Qahera News TV, which is close to the country's security agencies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN halts humanitarian work in Yemen's Houthi stronghold after staff detentions
item.description: The United Nations has suspended humanitarian operations in northern Saada after rebels detained eight more U.N. staffers, during one of the world's worst humanitarian crises.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian healthcare workers threaten Israeli man, claim to have killed Jews in their care on camera
item.description: Two Australian healthcare workers are accused of threatening an Israeli man via an online video chat, claiming they have killed patients based on nationality.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Freed American hostage Marc Fogel lands in US after years in Russian captivity
item.description: Marc Fogel, an American who had been detained in Russia since 2021, landed in the U.S. on Tuesday after his release from Russia following talks with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King of Jordan urges patience as Trump presses Gaza takeover
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday dug in on his vision for a U.S. takeover of the Gaza Strip as he sat alongside King Abdullah II of Jordan, who expressed reservations about the idea.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Ecuador's presidential election goes to runoff between conservative incumbent, leftist lawyer
item.description: Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election in April between conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González. Crime is a major issue for voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French girl, 11, found dead near school, murder investigation underway
item.description: Investigators in France have launched a murder investigation after a missing 11-year-old girl was found dead in a wooded area near her school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video: Rare ‘Black Demon’ fish spotted alive for first time
item.description: The scary-looking fish is usually found in water depths of 1,500 meters, where little to no sunlight reaches.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it's delaying next hostage release, claiming ceasefire violations
item.description: Hamas is delaying the next release of Oct. 7 hostages, accusing Israel of violating the terms of their ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump remains committed to US owning Gaza, says Middle East states could help rebuild war-torn area
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is committed to the U.S. purchasing and owning Gaza, but that he may allow Middle Eastern countries to rebuild sections of the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Marine identified as victim of surveillance flight that crashed in Philippines
item.description: Military officials identified a U.S. Marine who died in a plane crash in the Philippines last week as Sgt. Jacob M. Durham, a California native.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel prepares for it
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
